surface_forms: Latin as a live, spoken language
description: Indo-European language of the Italic branch
description_wiki: Latin (lingua Latina or Latinum) is a classical language belonging to the Italic branch of the Indo-European languages.

Greek (Modern Greek: ελληνικά, romanized: elliniká [eliniˈka] ; Ancient Greek: ἑλληνική, romanized: hellēnikḗ [helːɛːnikɛ́ː]) is an Indo-European language, constituting an independent Hellenic branch within the Indo-European language family.

entity: Philological Association of the Pacific Coast
surface_forms: Philological Association of the Pacific Coast
aliases: Philological Association of the Pacific Coast

description: public library system in California, USA
description_wiki: The Berkeley Public Library is the public library system for Berkeley, California.

description: library built with money donated by Scottish-American businessman and philanthropist Andrew Carnegie: 2,509 Carnegie libraries were built between 1883 and 1929
description_wiki: A Carnegie library is a library built with money donated by Scottish-American businessman and philanthropist Andrew Carnegie.
John Galen Howard (May 8, 1864 – July 18, 1931) was an American architect and educator who began his career in New York before moving to California.
World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: university system in California, United States
description_wiki: The University of California (UC) is a public land-grant research university system in the U.S. state of California.

description: American philosopher, psychologist, and educational reformer (1859–1952)
description_wiki: John Dewey (; October 20, 1859 – June 1, 1952) was an American philosopher, psychologist, and educational reformer.

John Muir ( MURE; April 21, 1838 – December 24, 1914), also known as "John of the Mountains" and "Father of the National Parks", was a Scottish-born American naturalist, author, environmental philosopher, botanist, zoologist, glaciologist, and early advocate for the preservation of wilderness in the United States.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.
The Golden Gate is a strait on the west coast of North America that connects San Francisco Bay and the Pacific Ocean.
